title: Steel imports down 30% in 2026 as tariffs bolster US production
summary: Section 232 tariffs have reduced foreign steel entering the United States by nearly 30% year-to-date in 2026, while domestic production has increased by nearly 5 million tons since the start of 2025. summary: U.S. steel manufacturers are planning near-term capacity additions to meet anticipated long-term growth, despite a recent slowdown in production levels. New production capacity is coming online as the industry looks toward positive global and domestic output forecasts for 2026.

title: United States Steel Production
summary: U.S. steel production increased to 7,500 thousand tonnes in May 2026, rising from 7,200 thousand tonnes in April. This marks a rebound after production remained unchanged at 7,200 thousand tonnes in April, according to data from the World Steel Association.

title: Copper up 9% since Iran war, near January peak. Will AI boom, shortage propel red metal to new highs?
summary: Copper prices are surging due to geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and booming AI-driven demand. title: Global Copper Prices Expected To Rise, Major Markets ...

title: Capstone Copper Announces Record 2025 Production Results and Provides Update on Mantoverde Labour Negotiations
summary: Capstone Copper reported a 22% increase in consolidated copper production compared to 2024, reaching a record annual total of 224,764 tonnes. The company achieved its annual production guidance for 2025, driven by the ramp-up of the Mantoverde Development, though operations there continue at 50-75% of normal levels due to an ongoing union strike.

title: The Copper Mining Market in 2026: Current and Future Trends
summary: Global copper mine production is projected to rise by approximately 2.3% in 2026, primarily due to expansions in Chile, Peru, Zambia, and a rebound in Indonesia. However, refined copper production growth is expected to decelerate to 0.9% in 2026 compared to 3.4% the previous year due to limited concentrate supply.

title: Can copper supply keep up with surging demand?
summary: Global copper demand is forecast to surge by 24% by 2035, but new mine supply is lagging as Western miners pull back while China increases investment. To balance the market in 2035, an estimated 8 million tonnes per annum of new mining capacity is required, alongside 3.5 million tonnes from direct scrap use.

title: Copper production faces stagnation despite high prices
summary: In 2025, the country recorded one of the lowest copper production levels of the last decade, with a 1.6% drop compared to the previous year. Production is expected to rise slightly to 5.6 million tonnes this year and 5.009 million tonnes in 2027, before falling again through 2030 due to declining ore grades and aging mining districts.

title: Green nickel for sustainable electrification
summary: Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Sustainable Materials developed a carbon-free, energy-saving method for nickel extraction using hydrogen plasma instead of carbon. This single-step process reduces CO2 emissions by 84% and is up to 18% more energy-efficient when powered by renewable electricity and green hydrogen[1].

title: Nickel – a mineral with a challenging role in clean tech
summary: Nickel is crucial for the net-zero transition with demand expected to grow 65% this decade, requiring more sustainable production methods to minimize environmental impact[2]. A meta-analysis confirms clean energy technologies are the fastest-growing sector for nickel demand[2].

title: Nickel Industry News, Views and Commentary

title: Prysmian shares rise as Italian cable maker seen as winner amid US copper tariffs

title: Prysmian Boosts Use of Recycled Copper in U.S. Amid Trade Tension
summary: Prysmian is increasingly incorporating recycled copper in the United States as Chinese buyers withdraw from the scrap market, according to its CEO. This strategic shift reflects how trade disputes between the U.S. and China are reshaping global commodity flows.
